summary: Matched a large block of src/melee/gm/gm_16AE.c by replacing placeholders with typed game-mode logic, expanding gm_16AE static structs, and tightening many related prototypes. The decomp.dev report credited the PR with +7040 matched bytes, 29 newly matched gm_16AE functions, and 6 additional unmatched-function improvements.
what_changed: Implemented many formerly stubbed or partially matched functions in gm_16AE.c, including fn_8016C4F4, gm_8016C5C0, gm_8016C658, gm_8016C6C0, gm_8016C75C, fn_8016C7F0, fn_8016CBE8, fn_8016CD98, fn_8016CF4C, gm_8016D32C_OnFrame, fn_8016D538, fn_8016DCC0, fn_8016E124, fn_8016E5C0, gm_8016E9C8, gm_8016F088, gm_8016F208, fn_8016F280, gm_8016F2F8, and fn_8016F344.

Expanded and reshaped gm_16AE static data types, especially lbl_8046B6A0_t and the newly forward-declared lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t, with nested fields such as x24C, x24C8, x58, and x44C used by the matched code.
Changed gm_8016B774 from returning UNK_T to returning struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, and updated it to return &lbl_8046B6A0.x24C.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in gm_1601.h with concrete prototypes, including void, bool, int, typed struct pointers, HSD_GObj*, StartMeleeData*, and PlayerInitData* signatures.
Adjusted a BSS symbol definition for lbl_8046DBC8 in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from size 0x10 to size 0xE, matching the static header declaration static u8 lbl_8046DBC8[0xe].
Added required includes to gm_16AE.c for newly referenced systems, including gm_1A36.h, gm_1B03.h, lblanguage.h, plbonus.h, plbonuslib.h, and math.h.
Replaced several raw pad button constants with named constants such as HSD_PAD_X and HSD_PAD_START.
Touched player, interface, menu, pause, gmmain, and related headers/call sites to keep signatures consistent with the new gm_16AE types.
smart_moves: The PR did not only paste function bodies; it first made the surrounding type information usable, which allowed many function signatures to become concrete and reduced reliance on UNK_T and UNK_PARAMS.
Introducing lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t as a forward-declared nested game-mode state type let functions pass precise substructure pointers instead of opaque global-state addresses.
Correcting lbl_8046DBC8's BSS size to 0xE aligned the symbols file with the inferred static declaration, avoiding a subtle data-layout mismatch.
Using HSD_PAD_X and HSD_PAD_START made input checks clearer while preserving the exact bit tests needed for matching.
Small static inline helpers and explicit dontinline wrappers were used around call sites such as fn_8016C46C and fn_8016CF4C, suggesting careful control of compiler output rather than broad rewrites.
PAD_STACK annotations were added in several newly matched functions, showing stack-shape preservation was handled deliberately.
decomp_lessons: Large gm module matches often depend on recovering the local BSS/global state layout first; once lbl_8046B6A0 substructures were typed, many access-heavy functions became practical to match.
Converting header prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to exact return and argument types can be part of the matching fix, not just cleanup, because call-site ABI and casts affect generated code.
When a function returns a pointer into a global struct, model the substructure as a real type rather than keeping UNK_T; this improves downstream function signatures and field access matching.
Named input constants are safe when they compile to the same masks and can improve readability without sacrificing match quality.
Do not overlook config/GALE01/symbols.txt when refining static data; inferred array sizes in C and linker-symbol metadata need to agree.
Partial progress is valuable: the bot reported several functions improved to 70-94% even though they were not fully matched in this PR.
naming_conventions: Address-based symbols were preserved for unknown game-mode functions, e.g. fn_8016C7F0, gm_8016D32C_OnFrame, and gm_8016E9C8.
OnEnter and OnFrame suffixes were used where lifecycle meaning was known, e.g. gm_8016E934_OnEnter and gm_8016D32C_OnFrame.
Recovered global/BSS struct names remained address-derived, e.g. lbl_8046B6A0_t and lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.
Unknown fields in recovered structs used x-offset style names such as x24C, x24C8, x58, and x44C rather than invented semantic names.
Game-mode public functions kept the gm_ prefix; local or unknown functions kept fn_ address-style naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with sizes such as 4, 8, 0x10, 0x14, and 0x28 to preserve stack layout in newly matched functions.
Used static inline helper functions for codegen shaping, including fn_8016CBE8_inline, fn_8016CFE0_inline, fn_8016D634_inline, gm_8016E9C8_inline, and gm_8016EDDC_inline.
Added explicit dontinline-style wrappers around calls such as fn_8016C46C_dontinline and fn_8016CF4C_dontinline to influence call structure.
Tightened signedness and width in prototypes, such as changing gm_80160854 parameters from s8 to u8 and changing some CharacterKind parameters to s8 where the diff showed that was required.
Used concrete bool/int/void return types for many functions previously declared as UNK_RET, reducing ABI ambiguity.
Matched global-state refresh idioms that compare x0 against gm_801A4BA8() and then call gm_80166378() before reading per-player fields.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 34.19%, a +0.18% improvement and +7040 bytes.
The bot reported 29 new 100% matches, all in main/melee/gm/gm_16AE.

The largest newly matched functions reported were fn_8016C7F0 (+632 bytes), gm_8016D32C_OnFrame (+524 bytes), gm_8016E9C8 (+504 bytes), fn_8016CD98 (+436 bytes), fn_8016CBE8 (+432 bytes), and fn_8016E124 (+408 bytes).
The bot also reported 6 improved but still unmatched functions: fn_8016CFE0, fn_8016E2BC, fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016D634, gm_8016EDDC, and gm_8016ECE8.
follow_up_queries: Look up later PRs that finish fn_8016CFE0, fn_8016E2BC, fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016D634, gm_8016EDDC, or gm_8016ECE8.
Search for future renames or semantic recovery of lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t and its fields x24C8, x58, and x44C.
Check whether the BSS size correction for lbl_8046DBC8 influenced adjacent globals lbl_8046DBD8 or lbl_8046DBE8 in later work.
Search for other gm_16AE matching PRs that reuse PAD_STACK plus static inline/dontinline wrapper tactics.
